message.content: o macos está reclamando de falta de espaço, já removi algumas coisas mas o 'system data' continua usando 190gb, pesquise os arquivos que mais interferem nisso, crie um plano de confirmação, mas não remova antes de eu confirmar

content.text: Vou pesquisar sobre o problema de "System Data" no macOS e criar um plano para identificar os arquivos que ocupam espaço.

content.content:
Web search results for query: "macOS System Data taking too much space 2025 how to reduce clear"

Links: [{"title":"Mac \"System Data\" Too Large? 10 Ways to Clear It (2026)","url":"https://www.drbuho.com/how-to/clear-system-storage-mac"},{"title":"How to clear System Data on Mac: 2025 guide (free up 50GB+)","url":"https://macpaw.com/how-to/clear-system-storage-mac"},{"title":"System data taking up 115GB - Apple Community","url":"https://discussions.apple.com/thread/254588225"},{"title":"MAC OS System Data using a lot of storage - Apple Community","url":"https://discussions.apple.com/thread/255463853"},{"title":"Free up storage space on Mac - Apple Support","url":"https://support.apple.com/en-us/102624"},{"title":"Why Mac System Data gets so large","url":"https://cleanmymac.com/blog/why-system-storage-takes-so-much-space"},{"title":"What To Do if macOS System Data is Huge/Large - The Mac Observer","url":"https://www.macobserver.com/macos/fix-macos-system-data-huge/"},{"title":"How to Clear System Data on Mac? Ways to Reduce Huge System Storage on macOS","url":"https://mackeeper.com/blog/mac-system-storage-cleaning/"},{"title":"How to delete System Data on a Mac | Macworld","url":"https://www.macworld.com/article/676493/how-to-delete-system-storage-on-mac.html"},{"title":"How to clear System Data on Mac storage - Detailed guide","url":"https://nektony.com/how-to/clean-up-system-storage-on-mac"}]

Based on the search results, here's how to reduce System Data that's taking up too much space on macOS:

## What is System Data on macOS?

The category named System Data (or Other) is a general category that measures the storage space used by all Apple and third-party files that don't belong to any more specific category.(no content)When you look closely at the storage on your Mac running macOS Tahoe, Sequoia, Sonoma, Ventura, or Monterey, you may notice a category labeled "System Data," which can range from 10 GB to over 100 GB.(no content)The default size of the macOS system is 12 GB. This implies that having around 12GB of system data is normal.

## How to Check System Data

Click the Apple menu > System Settings > General > Storage on macOS Ventura and later, or click the Apple menu > About This Mac > Storage on macOS Monterey and earlier.

## Methods to Reduce System Data

### 1. Clear Cache Files
The system and applications running on your Mac create cache files in the Library Folder. To free up more space, you can manually delete unnecessary cache files. Follow these steps: Click the Go menu in Finder and select Go to Folder.

### 2. Empty the Trash
Emptying the Trash on your Mac can also help reduce System Data. When you move files to the Trash, they are not completely deleted but remain on your drive, taking up valuable storage space. You can permanently delete them by emptying the Trash.

### 3. Remove Time Machine Snapshots
If there are snapshots created, they will grow in size based on the amount of data you deleted. Snapshots in macOS are of the entire User/Apps volume. Snapshots are accounted for in the System Data storage space. They can be VERY large.(no content)If you haven't created a backup in a while, a build-up of Time Machine snapshots could be responsible for a Mac system taking up too much space. So, you should back up your computer to remove System Data on your Mac and keep everything updated. Creating a new manual backup could solve your problem: Connect your external backup drive. Then go to System Settings > Time Machine, right-click the drive, and select the option to do a backup now.

### 4. Delete Old iOS/iPhone Backups
Old backups of your iPhone or iPad if you have backed them up to your Mac rather than iCloud can use unnecessary space on your Mac, so it's a good idea to clear them out once in a while.

### 5. Restart Your Mac
Over time, your Mac collects temporary files, caches, and other unnecessary data that can take up valuable storage space. A quick restart helps clear out these files, reset your apps, and stop any unresponsive background processes.

### 6. Boot in Safe Mode
To temporarily make more storage space available, it might help to start up your Mac in safe mode. Your Mac then clears certain system caches, which are automatically created again as needed.

### 7. Update macOS
If your Mac downloaded a new macOS update but didn't install it yet, it could be taking up a lot of system storage on your hard disk. The simplest solution is to update your Mac to the latest operating system, which clears out any updates you already downloaded and can lower System Data by removing cached installers.

### 8. Use Apple's Storage Optimization Features
Optimize Storage — If you click this option, macOS will automatically remove movies and TV shows that you've already viewed on your Mac. Empty Trash Automatically — Clicking this option will set your Mac to automatically delete files that have been in the Trash for more than 30 days.

### 9. Clean Downloads Folder
Delete files in your Downloads folder. Open this folder from the Dock, or by choosing Go > Downloads from the menu bar in the Finder.

## Important Notes

System Data cannot be completely deleted unless you wipe your Mac. Fortunately, we can reduce it to some extent. Try the following ten methods to make the System Data size smaller.(no content)You can't manage the contents of this category. The contents are managed by macOS, and the category varies in size depending on the current state of your Mac.(no content)If you delete things from System, System Data and macOS you are much more likely to damage your Mac. Always be careful when manually deleting system files and consider backing up your Mac first.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

input.command: find "$HOME/Library/Application Support" -maxdepth 1 -type d 2>/dev/null | while read dir; do du -sh "$dir" 2>/dev/null; done | sort -hr | head -15
for dir in ~/Library/"Application Support"/*/; do du -sh "$dir" 2>/dev/null; done | sort -hr | head -15
xcrun simctl list devices 2>/dev/null | grep -c "Booted\|Shutdown" || echo "Xcode não instalado"
ls -d ~/Library/"Application Support"/*/ 2>/dev/null | while read d; do echo "$(du -sh "$d" 2>/dev/null)"; done | sort -hr | head -20

newTodos.content: Identificar maiores consumidores de espaço em System Data
Analisar caches do sistema e aplicativos
Criar plano de limpeza para confirmação

content.text:
Análise completa. Aqui está o resumo e o plano de limpeza:

---

## 📊 Diagnóstico: Maiores Consumidores de Espaço

| Localização | Tamanho | Descrição |
|-------------|---------|-----------|
| `~/.npm` | **67 GB** | Cache do npm |
| `~/Library/Caches` | **59 GB** | Caches diversos |
| `~/Library/Developer` | **22 GB** | Xcode/Simuladores iOS |
| `~/Library/Application Support` | **20 GB** | Dados de apps |
| `/var/folders` | **4.1 GB** | Arquivos temporários |
| `~/Downloads` | **4.8 GB** | Downloads |

### Detalhamento dos Caches (59 GB):
| Pasta | Tamanho |
|-------|---------|
| JetBrains | 22 GB |
| Yarn | 21 GB |
| Google (Chrome) | 5.8 GB |
| pnpm | 3.4 GB |
| Playwright | 2.4 GB |
| CocoaPods | 1.4 GB |

### Simuladores iOS:
- **52 dispositivos** ocupando **19 GB**

---

## 🧹 Plano de Limpeza (Aguardando Confirmação)

### Nível 1 - Seguro (recomendado) - ~145 GB
| Ação | Comando | Economia Estimada |
|------|---------|-------------------|
| 1. Limpar cache npm | `npm cache clean --force` | ~67 GB |
| 2. Limpar cache Yarn | `yarn cache clean` | ~21 GB |
| 3. Limpar cache pnpm | `pnpm store prune` | ~3 GB |
| 4. Limpar JetBrains antigos | Remover `old-idea` + versões antigas | ~17 GB |
| 5. Limpar simuladores iOS não usados | `xcrun simctl delete unavailable` | ~10-15 GB |
| 6. Limpar cache Playwright | Remover pasta | ~2.4 GB |
| 7. Limpar cache Homebrew | `brew cleanup --prune=all` | ~0.5 GB |

### Nível 2 - Moderado (opcional)
| Ação | Comando | Economia |
|------|---------|----------|
| 8. Limpar dados Docker | `docker system prune -a` | ~5 GB |
| 9. Limpar Downloads antigos | Revisar manualmente | ~4.8 GB |
| 10. Limpar cache Chrome | Via Chrome ou pasta | ~5.8 GB |

---

**Qual nível você quer que eu execute?**
- **A)** Apenas Nível 1 (mais seguro)
- **B)** Nível 1 + Nível 2
- **C)** Itens específicos (me diga quais números)

Sources:
- [MacPaw - Clear System Storage](https://macpaw.com/how-to/clear-system-storage-mac)
- [Apple Support - Free up storage](https://support.apple.com/en-us/102624)

todos.content: Identificar maiores consumidores de espaço em System Data
Analisar caches do sistema e aplicativos
Criar plano de limpeza para confirmação

content.thinking:
O usuário quer executar o Nível 1 (itens 1 ao 7). Vou executar cada comando, mas preciso pedir confirmação antes de cada ação destrutiva conforme as regras de segurança. Na verdade, o usuário já confirmou que quer executar todos os itens do 1 ao 7, então posso prosseguir.

Vou executar os comandos em sequência:
1. npm cache clean --force
2. yarn cache clean
3. pnpm store prune
4. Remover JetBrains antigos (old-idea + versões antigas)
5. xcrun simctl delete unavailable
6. Remover cache Playwright
7. brew cleanup --prune=all

Vou começar executando os que são independentes em paralelo.

Excelente! O espaço disponível passou de 32 GB para 150 GB. Isso é um ganho de aproximadamente 118 GB!

Antes:
- /: 32Gi disponível

Depois:
- /: 150Gi disponível

O npm passou de 67 GB para 346 MB.
Os Caches passaram de 59 GB para 14 GB (removidos ~45 GB).

A limpeza foi bem sucedida!

content.text:
---

## ✅ Limpeza Concluída!

| Métrica | Antes | Depois | Liberado |
|---------|-------|--------|----------|
| **Espaço disponível** | 32 GB | **150 GB** | **+118 GB** |
| `~/.npm` | 67 GB | 346 MB | ~67 GB |
| `~/Library/Caches` | 59 GB | 14 GB | ~45 GB |

### Resumo das ações:
1. ✅ Cache npm limpo
2. ✅ Cache Yarn limpo (21 GB)
3. ✅ Cache pnpm limpo (397k arquivos removidos)
4. ✅ JetBrains antigos removidos (old-idea + WebStorm2025.1)
5. ✅ Simuladores iOS indisponíveis removidos
6. ✅ Cache Playwright removido
7. ✅ Homebrew limpo (~668 MB)

O "System Data" deve refletir a redução na próxima vez que você verificar em **Sistema > Geral > Armazenamento** (pode demorar alguns minutos para atualizar).

content.thinking: O usuário quer limpar os dados do Docker. Anteriormente vi que o Docker não estava rodando, mas tinha ~5GB em ~/Library/Containers/com.docker.docker. Vou verificar se o Docker está rodando agora e então executar a limpeza.

input.command: docker system df 2>/dev/null || echo "Docker não está rodando"

content.thinking:
O Docker não está rodando. Para limpar os dados do Docker, o ideal seria iniciar o Docker e executar `docker system prune -a`. Mas se o usuário quiser, posso sugerir remover diretamente a pasta de dados do Docker em ~/Library/Containers/com.docker.docker.

Vou perguntar ao usuário se quer iniciar o Docker ou remover os dados diretamente.

content.text:
O Docker não está rodando. Você tem duas opções:

**A)** Iniciar o Docker e executar `docker system prune -a` (limpa imagens, containers, volumes não usados)

**B)** Remover diretamente a pasta de dados (~5 GB em `~/Library/Containers/com.docker.docker`) - isso vai resetar completamente o Docker

Qual prefere?

summary: macOS Storage Cleanup: npm, Yarn, Docker Cache
